records.ety: [L. decrepitus, perhaps orig., noised out, noiseless, applied to old people, who creep about quietly; de- + crepare to make a noise, rattle: cf. F. dcrpit. See Crepitate.]

defs: Broken down with age; wasted and enfeebled by the infirmities of old age; feeble; worn out.
quotes: Already decrepit with premature old age.
